channel.title: Events Attended on Internship Report
channel.description: Recent content in Events Attended on Internship Report

item.title: Event 1: FCAJ Community Day
item.description:
FCAJ Community Day - May 2026

Event Information

Attributes Details
Event Name FCAJ Community Day - May
Time 09:00 AM - 12:00 PM, Saturday, May 23, 2026
Location Floor 26, Bitexco Financial Tower, 2 Hai Trieu St., Dist. 1, HCMC
Format Offline
Role Attendee / Intern
Organizer First Cloud AI Journey

Event Objectives

  • Update on the latest technology trends in Cloud Infrastructure (AWS) and Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI, LLMs).
  • Learn practical insights from top-tier experts on cost optimization, Multi-Agent architectures, and managing the stability of enterprise-grade AI models.
  • Expand professional networking within the engineer/developer community and members of the First Cloud AI Journey ecosystem.

List of Speakers / Organizers

  • Tinh Truong - Platform Engineer, GoTymeX
  • Pham Ng. Hai Anh - G-AsiaPacific Vietnam / AWS Community Builder
  • Nguyen Tuan Thinh - DevOps Engineer, First Cloud AI Journey
  • UTMorpho Team - Outstanding Participants at LotusHacks 2026
  • Duc Dao - Solution Architect, Cloud Kinetics
  • Vy Lam - Senior Business Systems Analyst, VPBank

Key Highlights

Non-Determinism of “Deterministic” LLM Settings (Speaker: Duc Dao - Cloud Kinetics)

  • Token Selection Mechanism: LLMs generate text token by token by converting logits into probabilities via a softmax function. Theoretically, setting Temperature = 0 triggers greedy decoding (argmax) — always selecting the single highest-probability token to guarantee 100% reproducible and deterministic outputs for the same prompt.
  • The Reality Gap: In practice, even with temp=0, LLMs can still return non-deterministic responses. This randomness does not stem from sampling, but rather from the underlying GPU architecture, floating-point calculation discrepancies, and various inference optimization algorithms utilized during hosting.
  • Impacts & Mitigation: Setting temp=0 often leads the model to get stuck in repetitive generation loops. A practical remedy is setting temp=0.1 as a “sweet spot”, which keeps the probability distribution extremely sharp (near-deterministic) while introducing a tiny stochastic nudge to prevent loop locks. Additionally, engineers should leverage higher repeat penalty values and fundamentally design downstream applications to tolerate variance from the start.

Enterprise-Grade Multi-Agent System: The Case of Startup Credit Scoring (Speaker: Vy Lam - VPBank)

  • The Structural Mismatch: Traditional banking credit scoring systems routinely fail to evaluate startups due to data misalignment. Traditional frameworks mandate 3+ years of audited financial statements, stable collateral, and predictable revenue patterns. In contrast, startup data is highly multi-dimensional, heavily unstructured, and volatile (6-18 months of operation, with core assets being IP, team composition, and market traction).
  • The Virtual Credit Committee Blueprint: To tackle multi-dimensional data, the architecture shifts from a Single Agent to a Multi-Agent paradigm. The system orchestrates multiple highly specialized agents: a Financial Agent (evaluating burn rate and runway), a Market Agent (analyzing TAM/SAM), a Team Agent (vetting founder backgrounds), and a Traction Agent. This distributed expertise delivers comprehensive insights and robust auditability that a single agent cannot match.
  • Enterprise Mindset & ROI: Transitioning AI from a working prototype to production-ready requires security and compliance to be implemented as a Day 1 mindset. The production architecture incorporates Cognito/OAuth2 for authentication, Bedrock Guardrails for content filtering, IAM least-privilege boundaries, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, all deployed via Terraform. This delivers a compelling business case with a 90%+ cost reduction and 95% faster processing speed.

Amazon Q Business: Leveraging Q Chat, Amazon Q Apps, custom web experiences, and Amazon Q in QuickSight to analyze data and automate operations using natural language.

Exploring CloudFront Foundation:

  • Deeply analyzed methods of optimizing static and dynamic content delivery across AWS’s edge network infrastructure.
  • Compared performance boundaries of CloudFront Functions (executing instantly at Edge Locations, suited for URL rewrites and basic header checks) vs. Lambda@Edge (running fully programmable, complex backend computational logic closer to regional endpoints).

LotusHacks Journey with UTMorpho:

  • Explored inspiring stories from developing and scaling the award-winning project at LotusHacks 2026.
  • Learned valuable teamwork skills, progress scheduling, and the rigorous transition process of converting an ambitious concept into a stable system operating on AWS.

Key Lessons Learned

Technical Domain

  • LLM Principles: Gained a deeper understanding of model non-determinism and the critical role of hyperparameter tuning in production environments.
  • Multi-Agent Architectures: Discovered how coordinating multiple specialized AI agents can systematically solve complex, domain-specific enterprise problems.
  • Security-First Integration: Realized that embedding security layers (Cognito, Bedrock Guardrails, least-privilege IAM) must be a Day 1 design priority.

Practical Skills

  • Learned how to optimize compute and cost margins when deploying AI systems at scale.
  • Discovered how to bridge academic theories with real-world enterprise applications in corporate finance.

Application to Work

  • Applying the Context Framework (Goals, Constraints, Criteria) to systematically interact with AI tools, dramatically improving daily task efficiency.
  • Strictly adhering to rigid Cloud IAM access controls and security boundary standards during upcoming enterprise internship assignments.

Personal Experience

Highlights

  • Strongly impressed by the Virtual Credit Committee architecture that elegantly solves the lack of historical startup financials.
  • The mathematical and hardware breakdown behind LLM repetitive loops clarified several major core engineering uncertainties.

Key Takeaways

  • The future does not pit Humans against AI, but rather humans who know how to engineer context against those who do not.
  • When pushing AI into production, stability, security boundaries, and data auditability hold far more weight than a flashy proof-of-concept demo.

Event Images

1 / 5

Conclusion

The May edition of FCAJ Community Day offered an all-encompassing perspective spanning from core cloud infrastructure up to intelligent enterprise logic layers. This pool of knowledge serves as a stellar foundation, setting up a proper architectural mindset for my internship and future career growth.

item.title: Event 2: FCAJ Community Day
item.description:
FCAJ Community Day - June 2026

Event Information

Attributes Details
Event Name FCAJ Community Day - June
Time 08:30 AM - 11:30 AM, Saturday, June 27, 2026
Location Bitexco Financial Tower, HCMC
Format Offline
Role Attendee / Intern
Organizer First Cloud AI Journey

Event Objectives

  • Update on emerging trends in Generative AI, Voice Agents, DevOps Agents, and cloud operations workflows on AWS.
  • Study how AI is applied to solve tangible business challenges such as conversational voice support, automated incident response, workforce planning, and secure enterprise data integration.
  • Deepen understanding of Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Nova Sonic, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and agentic architectures in building highly scalable, enterprise-ready systems.
  • Gain a practical, industry-centric perspective on executing AI solutions beyond simple static demos, focusing on real-world data and measurable business value.

Key Topics

  • Deep Response Engine: Bridging the gap between automated incident detection and resolution in modern cloud environments.
  • Voice Agents: Constructing highly conversational, human-like AI experiences at scale.
  • AWS DevOps Agent: Enhancing cloud operations, monitoring workflows, and driving down issue resolution cycles.
  • Amazon Q Business: Empowering enterprise productivity and supporting workforce optimization.
  • MCP Private Connection: Restricting AI interactions with internal enterprise tools and database models to secure private network pathways.

Key Highlights

Voice Agents: Building Human-Like AI Conversations at Scale (Speaker: Trung Vu - Revve AI)

This presentation was the highlight of the event for me. It did not merely showcase AI voice interfaces but provided a transparent look into the complex backend architectures and trade-offs required to operate Voice Agents at scale.
